purpose: Provenance and build pins for the vendored libghostty-vt headers and per-platform SIMD-off static archives linked by the `-tags ghostty` TUI build. Build-on-install machines need only a C compiler; Zig/CMake are required only to (re)produce the archives recorded here.

ghostty.pin_source: FetchContent GIT_TAG in go-libghostty's CMakeLists.txt at the binding commit above; the two pins must move together

headers.source: include/ghostty/ tree at the pinned ghostty commit, vendored unmodified (LICENSE alongside is ghostty's)

build.command: ./build-archives.sh (encodes the full recipe). NOT just `zig build -Demit-lib-vt`: that emits members Apple ld rejects as unaligned and omits the f128 quad-float compiler-rt builtins Zig's std formatting references. The script builds the SIMD-off vt archive, builds Zig's compiler_rt standalone for the target, then merges the main vt object with the full compiler_rt object — via Apple `libtool` (8-byte aligns) for darwin targets, `llvm-ar` for linux. Run with GHOSTTY_DIR=<checkout at ghostty.commit> ZIG=/opt/homebrew/opt/zig@0.15/bin/zig.
build.notes: SIMD off yields a fully static, zero-dependency archive so end-user machines link it with only a C compiler. The cgo CFLAGS/LDFLAGS contract lives in tui/internal/work/ghostty_link.go; the binding's pkg-config directive is satisfied by pkg-config-shim.sh. The merged-in compiler_rt provides __extenddftf2/__extendxftf2/__multf3/__trunctfdf2 (absent from Apple libclang_rt.osx.a; supplied by libgcc on Linux).
build.built_with: Homebrew zig@0.15 bottle (exactly 0.15.2, arm64_tahoe) on macOS 26.5 host (darwin/arm64). darwin_amd64 and linux_amd64 were cross-compiled from that host via -Dtarget; native codegen ran on arm64.
build.toolchain_caveat: The official ziglang.org zig-0.15.2 release binary CANNOT build on a macOS 26 host: its self-hosted MachO linker fails to resolve any libSystem symbol for native targets (only explicit cross-targets link). The Homebrew zig@0.15 bottle (built against Homebrew LLVM/LLD for macOS 26) links natively and was used here. ghostty requires exactly Zig 0.15.x (rejects 0.16), so use the Homebrew zig@0.15 bottle, or build on macOS <=15 / Linux, when reproducing these archives.
